steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice "most effectively uses information from the given sentences to present the study and its findings." A correct answer must do two things: name the researchers or study, and state the actual findings.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — incomplete sentence: the sentence cuts off before stating any finding. ✗
  • Choice B — names the researchers and states the findings: identifies who conducted the study, gives the total count of 146 springtail species, and breaks that down by genus (1 from Hemisotoma, 3 from Sphaeridia). ✓
  • Choice C — describes intent, not results: explains what the study aimed to find but never states the actual findings. ✗
  • Choice D — omits the researchers: gives some data but drops the study/researcher context entirely. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
